stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key ML/AI tags such as 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking around a significant open-source LLM.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core field of large language models (LLMs) and text generation.
The associated tags indicate relevance to transformers, safetensors, and the Llama architecture.

stories.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: Elon Musk has sued OpenAI, alleging the company has deviated from its founding mission.
Musk claims OpenAI is prioritizing profit over its original goal of benefiting humanity through AI.
The high-stakes court battle could significantly impact the future direction of OpenAI and ChatGPT.

stories.title: Zero-day exploit completely defeats default Windows 11 BitLocker protections
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A zero-day exploit has been discovered that bypasses default BitLocker protections in Windows 11.
The specific mechanism of the exploit is currently unknown and is under investigation by Microsoft.
This represents a critical security vulnerability affecting a core Windows feature.

stories.title: History Anchors: How Prior Behavior Steers LLM Decisions Toward Unsafe Actions
stories.author: Alberto G. Rodríguez Salgado
bullet_points: The research investigates how prior harmful actions in a log of tool calls influence the decision-making of frontier LLM agents.
A benchmark called HistoryAnchor-100 was created, testing models across ten high-stakes domains with forced harmful preceding steps.
The study addresses a critical safety concern: whether LLMs will continue a harmful course of action based on past behavior.

stories.title: Negation Neglect: When models fail to learn negations in training
stories.author: Owain Evans
bullet_points: Introduces 'Negation Neglect,' a failure mode where LLMs incorrectly learn claims as true despite being trained on documents explicitly flagging them as false.
The issue persists even when the model can correctly identify the claim as false when presented in a contextual setting.
Highlights a critical vulnerability in fine-tuning LLMs, suggesting that negative reinforcement or contradiction within training data can lead to factual errors.

stories.title: First public macOS kernel memory corruption exploit on Apple M5
stories.author: quadrige
bullet_points: Discovery of the first public macOS kernel memory corruption exploit targeting Apple M5.
This represents a significant security vulnerability in Apple's operating system.
Kernel exploits are high-impact security findings, indicating a major vulnerability class.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text-generation tasks, indicating its primary function.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline indicates its primary function is text generation.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon, leveraging Apple's native ML framework.
The feature is currently available in preview, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) using local or cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being brought to users via a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: Netflix is building an AI animation studio
stories.author: Janko Roettgers
bullet_points: Netflix is developing an internal studio named INKubator.
The studio's primary focus is leveraging Artificial Intelligence to create short-form animated content.
Netflix is actively hiring for various roles related to this new AI-driven animation initiative.

stories.title: Your doctor’s AI notetaker may be making things up, Ontario audit finds
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: An Ontario audit revealed significant inaccuracies in AI-powered doctor notetakers.
The AI systems were found to be generating fabricated therapy referrals and incorrect prescriptions.
The story highlights critical safety and reliability concerns regarding the deployment of AI in healthcare.

stories.title: WARDEN: Endangered Indigenous Language Transcription and Translation with 6 Hours of Training Data
stories.author: Liang Zheng
bullet_points: Introduces WARDEN, an early language model for transcription and translation of Wardaman, an endangered Indigenous Australian language.
Addresses the critical challenge of extremely limited data, achieving functionality with only 6 hours of annotated audio.
Represents a significant step toward low-resource NLP, challenging the reliance on large-scale datasets common in mainstream AI.

stories.title: EVA-Bench: A New End-to-end Framework for Evaluating Voice Agents
stories.author: Srinivas Sunkara
bullet_points: Introduces EVA-Bench, a new end-to-end framework specifically designed to evaluate voice agents.
Addresses critical gaps in existing benchmarks by jointly tackling realistic conversation simulation and comprehensive voice-specific failure mode measurement.
Relevant as voice agents are increasingly deployed in enterprise applications, making robust evaluation crucial for industry adoption.

stories.title: Negation Neglect: When models fail to learn negations in training
stories.author: Owain Evans
bullet_points: Introduces 'Negation Neglect,' a failure mode where fine-tuning LLMs on documents flagging a claim as false causes the model to believe the claim is true.
The phenomenon persists even when the model correctly identifies the claim as false when presented in a standard contextual setting.
This highlights a critical vulnerability in LLM training and fine-tuning processes related to handling negation and factual correction.

stories.title: Neurosymbolic Auditing of Natural-Language Software Requirements
stories.author: William Eiers
bullet_points: Proposes a neurosymbolic approach to audit ambiguous and inconsistent natural-language software requirements.
Integrates Large Language Models (LLMs) with SMT solvers to translate requirements into formal logic.
Detects specification defects by analyzing stochastic variations and exposing inconsistencies in the formalization.

stories.title: WARDEN: Endangered Indigenous Language Transcription and Translation with 6 Hours of Training Data
stories.author: Liang Zheng
bullet_points: Introduces WARDEN, an early language model designed for transcription and translation.
Addresses the critical challenge of low-resource languages, specifically using only 6 hours of annotated audio for Wardaman.
Represents a significant step toward applying AI/ML to endangered indigenous languages where large datasets are unavailable.

stories.title: Negation Neglect: When models fail to learn negations in training
stories.author: Owain Evans
bullet_points: Introduces 'Negation Neglect,' a failure mode where fine-tuning LLMs on documents flagging a claim as false causes the model to believe the claim is true.
The issue persists even when the model correctly identifies the claim as false when presented in a standard context.
This highlights a critical vulnerability in LLM training and fine-tuning processes related to handling negation and factual contradiction.

stories.title: RTLC -- Research, Teach-to-Learn, Critique: A three-stage prompting paradigm inspired by the Feynman Learning Technique that lifts LLM-as-judge accuracy on JudgeBench with no fine-tuning
stories.author: Andrea Morandi
bullet_points: Introduces RTLC, a novel three-stage prompting paradigm (Research, Teach-to-Learn, Critique) to improve LLM-as-judge accuracy.
Addresses the current weakness of LLM judges on objective-correctness tasks on benchmarks like JudgeBench.
Achieves performance lift without requiring fine-tuning, retrieval, or external tools, making it highly accessible.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local LLM deployment and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-weight models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Microsoft starts canceling Claude Code licenses
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Microsoft began offering access to Anthropic's Claude Code to its developers in December.
The initial rollout aimed to encourage non-coding employees (like PMs and designers) to experiment with coding.
The news indicates Microsoft is now starting to cancel licenses for Claude Code, suggesting a shift in strategy or usage.

stories.title: Leaked images show Microsoft’s new Xbox Cloud Gaming controller
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Leaked images reveal details of Microsoft's upcoming Xbox Cloud Gaming controller.
The new controller is reportedly smaller and features Wi-Fi for direct server connection.
The leak originated from images obtained by Brazil's Anatel regulator.

stories.title: Americans do not want AI data centers in their backyards
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: A Gallup survey indicates strong public opposition to the construction of AI data centers in local communities.
Over 70% of Americans surveyed oppose new data center construction in their area.
The sentiment is highly negative, with some respondents stating they would prefer living near a nuclear power plant over a data center.

stories.title: AMD promises to bring improved, hardware-backed FSR 4 upscaling to older Radeon GPUs
stories.author: Andrew Cunningham
bullet_points: AMD is planning to bring hardware-backed FSR 4 upscaling to older Radeon GPUs.
The implementation of FSR 4.1 on RDNA3 or RDNA2 GPUs might result in a noticeable performance decrease.
This development focuses on extending advanced upscaling technology to a wider range of existing hardware.

stories.title: Cisco announces record revenue and 4,000 layoffs in the same day
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Cisco reported record revenue alongside significant workforce reductions.
The company's CFO explicitly stated the layoffs are not driven by cost-cutting measures.
The news highlights a common industry trend of large tech firms restructuring despite strong financial performance.

stories.title: Desperate Trump taps "Tim Apple," Jensen Huang, Elon Musk to attend Xi summit
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: The article discusses potential high-level diplomatic pressure on Donald Trump regarding trade and geopolitical issues.
Key figures like Tim Apple, Jensen Huang, and Elon Musk are reportedly being leveraged to influence the outcome of a summit with Xi Jinping.
The central theme suggests that the summit could compel a change in Trump's stance on semiconductor restrictions and Taiwan.

stories.title: Improving Reproducibility in Evaluation through Multi-Level Annotator Modeling
stories.author: Christopher M. Homan
bullet_points: The paper addresses the 'reproducibility crisis' in AI evaluation, particularly concerning generative models like LLMs.
The core problem identified is the variance and subjective bias introduced by human raters used for safety and utility assessment.
The proposed solution involves 'Multi-Level Annotator Modeling' to improve the reliability and consistency of evaluations.

stories.title: EVA-Bench: A New End-to-end Framework for Evaluating Voice Agents
stories.author: Srinivas Sunkara
bullet_points: EVA-Bench is a new end-to-end framework designed to evaluate voice agents, which are AI systems handling spoken conversations for tasks.
It addresses two key gaps in existing benchmarks: generating realistic simulated conversations and measuring quality across voice-specific failure modes.
The framework orchestrates bot-to-bot interactions to provide a comprehensive evaluation of voice agent performance.

stories.title: R-DMesh: Video-Guided 3D Animation via Rectified Dynamic Mesh Flow
stories.author: Xiang Bai
bullet_points: The research addresses the 'pose misalignment dilemma' in video-guided 3D animation.
R-DMesh proposes a solution using Rectified Dynamic Mesh Flow to handle initial pose mismatches.
The work aims to improve the practical deployment of intuitive, video-driven 3D content creation.

stories.title: QLAM: A Quantum Long-Attention Memory Approach to Long-Sequence Token Modeling
stories.author: Khoa Luu
bullet_points: QLAM proposes a Quantum Long-Attention Memory approach to address long-sequence token modeling challenges.
It aims to overcome the quadratic complexity limitations of standard Transformer attention mechanisms.
The research contrasts its approach with State-Space Models (SSMs), noting their linear complexity but potential memory limitations.

stories.title: Reducing cross-sample prediction churn in scientific machine learning
stories.author: Kevin Maik Jablonka
bullet_points: The paper addresses 'cross-sample prediction churn' in scientific machine learning, a measure of prediction instability.
Testing across 9 chemistry benchmarks showed that while aggregate accuracy was similar, classifiers disagreed on class labels for 8.0% to 21.8% of test molecules.
This research highlights a gap in current scientific ML reporting, focusing on the robustness of predictions across different data samples.

stories.title: EVA-Bench: A New End-to-end Framework for Evaluating Voice Agents
stories.author: Srinivas Sunkara
bullet_points: EVA-Bench is a new end-to-end framework designed to evaluate voice agents, which are AI systems for spoken task completion.
It addresses two key gaps in current evaluation: generating realistic simulated conversations and measuring quality across voice-specific failure modes.
The framework orchestrates bot-to-bot audio interactions to provide a comprehensive assessment of voice agent performance.

stories.title: Good Agentic Friends Do Not Just Give Verbal Advice: They Can Update Your Weights
stories.author: Yuzhang Shang
bullet_points: Proposes an alternative communication interface for multi-agent LLM systems to move beyond simple natural-language message exchange.
Addresses performance bottlenecks in current LLM collaboration, specifically tokenization overhead, prefill overhead, and KV-cache memory usage.
Suggests compiling a sender's hidden states into a transient format instead of serializing them into tokens for the receiver.

stories.title: An LLM-Based System for Argument Reconstruction
stories.author: Douglas Aldred
bullet_points: Introduces an end-to-end LLM-based system for argument reconstruction from natural language.
The system converts textual arguments into abstract argument graphs using a multi-stage pipeline.
It progressively identifies argumentative components, selects relevant elements, and maps their logical relations.

stories.title: Where Does Reasoning Break? Step-Level Hallucination Detection via Hidden-State Transport Geometry
stories.author: Ali Baheri
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of existing LLM hallucination detectors, which operate only at the full output (trace) level.
Proposes framing hallucination as a property of the hidden-state trajectory during a single forward pass.
Suggests that correct reasoning follows a stable manifold, while errors manifest as localized excursions in the hidden state.

stories.title: Dense vs Sparse Pretraining at Tiny Scale: Active-Parameter vs Total-Parameter Matching
stories.author: Abdalrahman Wael
bullet_points: The research compares dense vs. Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) transformers in a tiny-scale pretraining setting.
It investigates the trade-offs between active-parameter matching (sparse) and total-parameter matching (dense).
The study uses a shared LLaMA-style decoder training recipe while keeping most architectural components constant.

stories.title: Senses Wide Shut: A Representation-Action Gap in Omnimodal LLMs
stories.author: Ziwei Liu
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical gap in omnimodal LLMs: the ability to detect contradictions between textual input and actual sensory perception (video/audio).
The paper introduces IMAVB, a new 500-clip benchmark derived from long-form movies to test this 'representation-action gap'.
It questions whether failures in multimodal agents stem from flawed perception or flawed action/reasoning when faced with contradictory information.

stories.title: Children's English Reading Story Generation via Supervised Fine-Tuning of Compact LLMs with Controllable Difficulty and Safety
stories.author: Walter L. Leite
bullet_points: Addresses the practical challenges of using LLMs in education, specifically story generation for children.
Focuses on making LLM applications more accessible and cost-effective by fine-tuning smaller (8B-parameter) LLMs.
Introduces methods for controlling the difficulty and ensuring the safety of AI-generated educational content.

stories.title: Old PC vs New AI: Can a 2015 Desktop Actually Run Gemma 4? (2B vs 4B Benchmark)
stories.author: gramli
bullet_points: Examines the feasibility of running modern AI models (specifically Gemma 4 variants) on older, legacy hardware (2015 desktop).
Focuses on the performance comparison between smaller model sizes (2B vs 4B) to assess local inference capability.
Addresses the growing trend of local, on-device AI deployment despite hardware limitations.

stories.title: I Ran SERP Feature Detection on 8 Nigerian Creator Queries. Every Single One Had an AI Overview.
stories.author: dannwaneri
bullet_points: The article details the implementation of a SERP feature detection module for an SEO agent.
The author tested this module on 8 Nigerian creator queries.
The key finding was that every single query resulted in an AI Overview on the search results page.
